description: Regular expression matching operations. Provides Perl-style regular expression patterns for searching, matching, and manipulating strings.

functions.name: compile

params.name: flags
params.description: Combination of re flags (e.g., re.IGNORECASE | re.MULTILINE)

returns.description: Compiled regular expression object that can be used for matching

functions.description: Compile a regular expression pattern into a RegexObject. Using compiled patterns is more efficient when the expression will be used several times.

functions.name: search

params.name: string
params.description: String to search for a match

returns.description: A MatchObject if the pattern matches anywhere in the string, or None if no match is found

functions.description: Scan through string looking for a location where the regular expression pattern produces a match. Returns None if no position in the string matches.

functions.name: match

params.name: string
params.description: String to match at the beginning

returns.description: A MatchObject if zero or more characters at the beginning of string match, or None

functions.description: Try to apply the pattern at the start of the string. Returns None if the string does not match the pattern. Note: this only matches at the beginning; use search() to match anywhere.

functions.name: findall

params.name: string
params.description: String to search for all matches

returns.description: List of all non-overlapping matches as strings. If the pattern has groups, returns a list of tuples of groups.

functions.description: Return all non-overlapping matches of pattern in string, as a list of strings. If groups are present, returns a list of tuples containing all groups.

functions.name: finditer

params.name: string
params.description: String to search for all matches

returns.description: Iterator yielding MatchObject instances for each non-overlapping match

functions.description: Return an iterator yielding MatchObject instances for all non-overlapping matches of pattern in string. The string is scanned left-to-right and matches are returned in the order found.

functions.name: sub

params.name: repl
params.description: Replacement string or a function that takes a MatchObject and returns the replacement string. Backreferences like \1 or \g<name> are supported in strings.

params.name: count
params.description: Maximum number of replacements. 0 (default) means replace all occurrences.

returns.description: String with all (or count) occurrences of pattern replaced by repl

functions.description: Return the string obtained by replacing the leftmost non-overlapping occurrences of pattern in string by the replacement repl.

functions.name: subn

params.name: count
params.description: Maximum number of replacements (0 means all)

returns.description: A 2-tuple (new_string, number_of_substitutions_made)

functions.description: Perform the same operation as sub(), but return a tuple (new_string, number_of_substitutions_made).

functions.name: split

params.name: maxsplit
params.description: Maximum number of splits. 0 (default) means no limit.

returns.description: List of strings split by pattern occurrences. If the pattern has capturing groups, their text is also included.

functions.description: Split string by the occurrences of pattern. If capturing parentheses are used in pattern, the text of all groups are also returned as part of the resulting list.

functions.name: escape

returns.description: String with all non-alphanumeric characters backslash-escaped

functions.description: Return string with all non-alphanumerics backslashed. Useful to match an arbitrary literal string that may have regex metacharacters in it.

classes.name: MatchObject
classes.description: Match objects are returned by search() and match() when a match is found. They contain information about the match and provide methods for accessing captured groups.

methods.name: group

params.name: group1
params.description: Group index (0 is entire match) or group name. Multiple arguments return a tuple.

returns.description: The matched substring for the given group(s). Returns None for groups that did not participate in the match.

methods.description: Returns one or more subgroups of the match. group(0) returns the entire match. group(1) returns the first parenthesized subgroup.

methods.name: groups

params.name: default
params.description: Value to use for groups that did not participate in the match (default None)

returns.description: Tuple containing all subgroups of the match

methods.description: Return a tuple containing all subgroups of the match, from 1 up to however many groups are in the pattern.

methods.name: groupdict

params.name: default
params.description: Value to use for groups that did not participate in the match

returns.description: Dictionary mapping group names to matched strings

methods.description: Return a dictionary containing all named subgroups of the match, keyed by the subgroup name.

methods.name: start

returns.description: Start position of the match, or -1 if the group did not participate

methods.description: Return the index of the start of the substring matched by group.

methods.name: end

returns.description: End position of the match, or -1 if the group did not participate

methods.description: Return the index of the end of the substring matched by group.

methods.name: span

returns.description: A 2-tuple (start, end) for the given group

methods.description: Return a tuple (m.start(group), m.end(group)) for the given group.

methods.name: expand

returns.description: String obtained by substituting backreferences in the template

methods.description: Return the string obtained by doing backslash substitution on the template string, as done by the sub() method.

fields.name: string
fields.description: The string passed to match() or search()

fields.name: re
fields.description: The regular expression object whose match() or search() produced this MatchObject

fields.name: pos
fields.description: The value of pos passed to search() or match()

fields.name: endpos
fields.description: The value of endpos passed to search() or match()

fields.name: lastindex
fields.description: The integer index of the last matched capturing group, or None

fields.name: lastgroup
fields.description: The name of the last matched capturing group, or None

classes.name: RegexObject
classes.description: Compiled regular expression object returned by re.compile(). Supports the same methods as the module-level functions but pre-compiled for efficiency.

methods.name: search

params.name: string
params.description: String to search for a match

params.name: pos
params.description: Index in the string where the search starts (default 0)

params.name: endpos
params.description: Index in the string beyond which the RE engine will not go

returns.description: A MatchObject if the pattern matches, or None

methods.description: Scan through string looking for a location where the regular expression produces a match.

methods.name: match

params.name: string
params.description: String to match at the beginning

params.name: pos
params.description: Index in the string where the match must start (default 0)

params.name: endpos
params.description: Index in the string beyond which the RE engine will not go

returns.description: A MatchObject if the pattern matches at pos, or None

methods.description: Try to apply the pattern at the start of the string. If pos is given, matching starts at that index.

methods.name: findall

params.name: string
params.description: String to search for all matches

params.name: endpos
params.description: Index beyond which the RE engine will not go

returns.description: List of all non-overlapping matches

methods.description: Return all non-overlapping matches of pattern in string, as a list of strings or tuples.

methods.name: finditer

params.name: string
params.description: String to search for all matches

params.name: endpos
params.description: Index beyond which the RE engine will not go

returns.description: Iterator yielding MatchObject instances

methods.description: Return an iterator yielding MatchObject instances for all non-overlapping matches.

methods.name: sub

params.name: count
params.description: Maximum number of replacements (0 means all)

returns.description: String with matches replaced

methods.description: Return the string obtained by replacing the leftmost non-overlapping occurrences of the pattern.

methods.name: subn

params.name: count
params.description: Maximum number of replacements (0 means all)

returns.description: A 2-tuple (new_string, number_of_substitutions_made)

methods.description: Perform the same operation as sub(), but return a tuple (new_string, number_of_subs_made).

methods.name: split

params.name: maxsplit
params.description: Maximum number of splits (0 means no limit)

returns.description: List of strings split by pattern occurrences

methods.description: Split string by the occurrences of the pattern.

fields.name: groups
fields.description: The number of capturing groups in the pattern

fields.name: groupindex
fields.description: Dictionary mapping group names to group numbers

fields.name: pattern
fields.description: The pattern string from which the RE object was compiled

constants.name: IGNORECASE
constants.description: Perform case-insensitive matching. Expressions like [A-Z] will match lowercase letters too.

constants.name: MULTILINE
constants.description: Make ^ match at the beginning of each line (after each newline) and $ match at the end of each line (before each newline).

constants.name: DOTALL
constants.description: Make the '.' special character match any character including a newline. Without this flag, '.' matches anything except a newline.

constants.name: VERBOSE
constants.description: Allow verbose regular expressions with comments and whitespace for readability. Whitespace within the pattern is ignored except when in a character class or preceded by an unescaped backslash.

constants.name: UNICODE
constants.description: Make \w, \W, \b, \B, \d, \D, \s, and \S dependent on the Unicode character properties database.
